extraction.one_line_summary: BENNETT Sam won sprint stage 2 in Netherlands after POPPEL Danny delivered superior lead-out in final 200m, beating PEDERSEN Mads and EEKHOFF Nils
tactical_patterns: POPPEL Danny's 200m lead-out for BENNETT proved superior to Trek's train for PEDERSEN despite less team depth.
Jumbo-Visma let EEKHOFF take race lead via stage position rather than defending GESINK's jersey, revealing GC priorities elsewhere.
Early KOM breakaway with VANPOPPEL/LOPEZ showed sprinters' teams willing to concede mountains jersey completely on flat Vuelta stages.

rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: positive: First win overcoming confidence crisis after missing Tour; now stage winner in four consecutive Grand Tours since 2018
rider_signals.POPPEL Danny: positive: Elite lead-out execution in final 200m suggests BENNETT has better sprint support than his recent results indicated
rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: neutral: Second place despite Trek lead-out train indicates he needs perfect positioning to beat top sprinters with stronger pilots
rider_signals.EEKHOFF Nils: neutral: Fourth on stage gains red jersey as Jumbo-Visma's expendable GC placeholder, team prioritizing later mountain stages
rider_signals.GROVES Kaden: negative: Only 10th suggests Alpecin sprint train malfunctioned or positioning failed badly on straightforward flat finish
